subparts.text: Explain any two criteria for selecting a good research problem. 1
Define skewness. What does positive skewness indicate? 1
Give two examples each of parametric and non-parametric tests. 2
State any two characteristics of a good research report. 2
Differentiate between a one-tailed and two-tailed hypothesis test. 3
What is a multiple regression analysis? Give an example. 3
List any four types of probability sampling techniques 4
Mention any two points to be kept in mind while writing a conclusion in a research report. 4

questions.question_text: Discuss the different types of research methods with suitable examples. 1
Describe the process of data collection. Highlight the factors influencing the choice of data collection method. 1
What is regression analysis? Explain the difference between simple and multiple regression with examples. 2

A researcher collected salary data from a company and found that the data was highly positively skewed.  Mean salary = ₹70,000  Median salary = ₹55,000  Standard Deviation = ₹25,000  Coefficient of variation = ?  Pearson’s skewness coefficient = ? Calculate both the coefficient of variation and Pearson's skewness coefficient, and interpret the distribution characteristics. 2
Differentiate between Type I and Type II errors. How can researchers minimize them? 3

Suppose a company claims that its new drug is effective in 80% of patients. A researcher tests the drug on a random sample of 100 patients and finds 75 of them were cured. Perform a hypothesis test at 5% significance level. Should the researcher accept the company's claim? 3
What are statistical fallacies? Explain any four common statistical fallacies researchers should avoid. 4

Develop an outline of a research report on the problem of HIGH DROPOUT RATE IN HIGHER EDUCATION. You may take help from the following points:  Significance, Scope, variables, methodology and implication of the study 4
